goal: Teach woof dispatch to write raw subprocess stdout to a gitignored audit/raw/ path, run a deterministic post-processor that redacts secrets and strips command_execution aggregated_output bloat from the committed audit, and apply a per-file cap as a safety net (tail-truncate strategy) with operator-configurable knobs in .woof/agents.toml.

stories.title: Audit policy contract + config loader
stories.intent: Land the [audit] block on agents.schema.json and a small loader that surfaces enabled/max_bytes/redact_patterns from .woof/agents.toml as a typed config. This is the contract surface S2 consumes; no behavioural change yet.

stories.title: Redactor + deterministic post-processor wired into woof dispatch
stories.intent: Wire raw-then-summarise audit handling into woof dispatch. Raw subprocess stdout writes to gitignored .woof/epics/E*/audit/raw/<role>-<ts>.output. A deterministic post-processor (1) applies conservative secret redaction; (2) for harnesses that emit JSONL with command_execution events (codex), rewrites those events to keep the command + exit_code + output length but drops the inlined aggregated_output content; (3) extracts the harness's terminal result text into the .meta file's result_text field; (4) writes the post-processed result to the committed .woof/epics/E*/audit/<role>-<ts>.output. The configured cap (default 256 KB) acts as a safety net only — when it engages, the strategy is tail-truncate (keep last max_bytes; footer points at audit/raw/). Realises O1 (redaction), O3 (configurable), O4 (deterministic summary + safety-net cap). O2 is deprecated and not implemented.
